title: ReAct vs plan-and-execute agents
description: ReAct interleaves reasoning and acting step by step; plan-and-execute drafts a full plan first then runs it. They fail differently.
body: Use when choosing an agent architecture. ReAct interleaves a thought, an action, and an observation each step, adapting as results arrive; plan-and-execute produces a multi-step plan up front and then executes it, optionally replanning. ReAct is flexible but can wander or loop; plan-execute is auditable but brittle if early observations invalidate the plan. Do not assume one pattern suits every task, and avoid running plan-execute without replanning hooks. Actions: pick by task predictability and add a no-progress guard either way. Validation signals include tests that ReAct halts on a stall and that plan-execute replans when an early step fails. Risk boundary: a rigid plan that keeps executing after a failed precondition can cause harm; require replanning or a gate.
